edges.description: Ischemia in the anterior spinal artery territory injures anterior horn cells and long tracts.
The acute vascular/ischemic event is frequently heralded by sudden back or neck pain at the level of the infarction.
Acute interruption of anterior spinal cord perfusion produces severe deficits that reach their nadir rapidly, typically within hours.
edges.target: Rapid nadir of severe neurologic deficits

edges.description: Injury to the corticospinal tracts and anterior horn motor neurons produces bilateral motor weakness below the lesion.
Injury to the anterolateral spinothalamic tracts produces dissociated loss of pain and temperature sensation with preserved proprioception.
edges.target: Loss of pain and temperature sensation

edges.description: Injury to lateral horn autonomic pathways disrupts bladder control, producing urinary incontinence.

edges.source: Perfusion-optimization protocol after high-risk aortic repair

nodes.description: Acute axial pain can accompany the hyperacute onset of anterior spinal artery syndrome and helps distinguish spinal cord infarction from slower inflammatory myelopathies.

evidence.reference_title: Anterior Spinal Artery Syndrome due to Intervertebral Disc Herniation
evidence.snippet: Motor weakness with or without pain was the most frequent presenting symptom accompanying bowel or bladder incontinence (25%) or diminished pain and temperature sensation with spared dorsal column sensation.

nodes.description: Ischemia in the anterior spinal cord damages anterior horn cells, corticospinal tracts, spinothalamic tracts, and lateral horn autonomic pathways, explaining motor, pain-temperature, and bladder or bowel findings.

evidence.reference_title: Incomplete Anterior Spinal Artery Syndrome Responsive to Intrathecal Baclofen.
evidence.snippet: the underlying neural structures responsible for these symptoms include the corticospinal tracts and anterior horns, anterolateral spinothalamic tracts, and lateral horns, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Histological Findings After Aortic Cross-Clamping in Preclinical Animal Models
evidence.snippet: Our main finding is that damage is predominantly in the grey matter of the spinal cord, although white matter damage in the spinal cord is also reported.

nodes.description: Occlusion, hypoperfusion, or procedure-associated interruption of anterior spinal artery territory blood flow injures the anterior spinal cord, producing the characteristic acute myelopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Incomplete Anterior Spinal Artery Syndrome Responsive to Intrathecal Baclofen.
evidence.snippet: Anterior cord syndrome (ACS) occurs as a result of ischemia in the territory of the anterior spinal artery (ASA).

evidence.reference_title: Spinal Cord Infarction: Clinical and Neuroradiological Clues of a Rare Stroke Subtype
evidence.snippet: Several etiologies can be considered, considering traditional vascular risk factors and diseases affecting the aorta and its main branches

nodes.description: Antiplatelet therapy such as aspirin is commonly considered when ASAS is managed as spinal cord infarction, although ASAS-specific randomized evidence is limited and treatment depends on the underlying cause.
Spinothalamic tract involvement causes bilateral loss of pain and temperature sensation.
nodes.id: Loss of pain and temperature sensation

evidence.reference_title: Incomplete Anterior Spinal Artery Syndrome Responsive to Intrathecal Baclofen.
evidence.snippet: bilateral loss of pain and temperature sensation

nodes.description: Acute paraparesis or paraplegia is a typical motor manifestation of anterior spinal artery territory infarction.

evidence.reference_title: Incomplete Anterior Spinal Artery Syndrome Responsive to Intrathecal Baclofen.
evidence.snippet: The typical presentation of an ASA stroke is paraparesis or paraplegia

nodes.description: In aortic-repair settings, management and prevention focus on maintaining spinal cord perfusion pressure, adequate hemoglobin, early limb reperfusion, neurologic monitoring, and selective or routine cerebrospinal fluid drainage depending on procedural risk.
nodes.id: Perfusion-optimization protocol after high-risk aortic repair

evidence.reference_title: A dedicated preventive protocol sustainably avoids spinal cord ischemia after endovascular aortic repair
evidence.snippet: The preventive protocol consisted of staging extensive aortic repairs, maintaining a mean arterial pressure (MAP) >80 mm Hg, Hb level >110 g/L, early lower limb reperfusion and neurological control per hour during the post-operative stay in the intensive care unit (36–72 h).

evidence.reference_title: Results of a multidisciplinary spinal cord ischemia prevention protocol in elective repair of Crawford's extent I-III thoracoabdominal aneurysm by fenestrated and branched endografts
evidence.snippet: A dedicated multidisciplinary SCI prevention protocol in elective F/B-EVAR for Crawford’s I-III TAAAs is feasible and safe, with encouraging rates of SCI (8% overall SCI, 6% permanent impairment, and 3% paraplegia).

nodes.description: Severe deficits usually develop rapidly, which helps distinguish spinal cord infarction from inflammatory myelopathy.
nodes.id: Rapid nadir of severe neurologic deficits

evidence.reference_title: Spinal Cord Infarction: Clinical and Neuroradiological Clues of a Rare Stroke Subtype
evidence.snippet: The strongest predictor of SCI diagnosis is a clinical variable, i.e., a time to nadir of severe deficits < 12 h.

nodes.description: Persistent deficits may require neurorehabilitation and symptom-directed management of spasticity or instability.

evidence.reference_title: Incomplete Anterior Spinal Artery Syndrome Responsive to Intrathecal Baclofen.
evidence.snippet: presenting with delayed-onset spasticity and instability several months following EVAR, who was subsequently treated with intrathecal baclofen.

nodes.description: Autonomic pathway injury can cause urinary dysfunction.
